description_long: This house features a combination of brick and siding exterior walls, with a complex roofline that includes both gable and flat roof sections. The structure is multi-leveled, with a prominent two-story section and an attached single-story wing. Windows are arranged in a mix of symmetrical and asymmetrical patterns, with several large rectangular panes and some smaller, higher-placed windows. The roof is primarily dark gray, contrasting with the reddish-brown brickwork and the green lawn base. The main entryway is set beneath a gabled roof, with a simple door and a small overhang, and there are multiple visible doors and access points around the building. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and modern residential architectural elements.
description: A multi-level brick house with a mix of gable and flat roofs, featuring varied window arrangements and a prominent entryway under a gabled roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main two-story brick section, large windows, and the attached single-story wing with a dark gable roof.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the gabled roof sections, rear entry, and the transition between the two-story and single-story parts.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the house, a smaller entryway, and the flat roof sections on the upper level.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the two-story brick facade, smaller windows, and the flat roof area.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with a mix of large and small rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry under a gabled roof with a simple door and small overhang, minimal decorative elements
